subject: [PATCH v3 00/10] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the current implementation has a potential performance issue due to multiple buffer pools, and suggested using a per-ring freelist for each buffer size instead, which would simplify allocation and reduce overhead.

reviewer noted that the bufpool layer would still allow allocating and freeing single buffers, but questioned whether additional complexity or performance overhead for multi-bufpool support would be significant
sentiment_signals: requested clarification on potential complexity/performance impact
reviews.raw_body:
I don't think this is an issue.  Allocation should be as simple as
keeping per-ring freelist for each buffer size that would have all
free buffers across an arbitrary number of buf pools.

 I don't think it would add much additional complexity, given

---

To conclude: bufpools allow allocating/freeing multiple buffers
together.  The API is a superset, since allocating/freeing a single
buffer is still possible.   Additional complexity or performance for
the bufpool layer should not be significant.

Is that correct?

If yes, I'd go with bufpools.

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested an alternative implementation for managing multiple buffer pools, where each pool is represented by an array entry in a separate config-defined array, and proposed modifying the fuse_uring_ent_in_out function to set the corresponding indices.
reviews.raw_body:
Really sorry for late replies.

Couldn't we make a config SQE define a max array size and then every
array entry would be something like

struct buf_entry {
	char *payload;
	int buf_pool_idx; /* which buf pool */
	int idx; /* index within the buf pool */	
}


And then in fuse_uring_ent_in_out you would need to set buf_pool_idx and
buf_idx


Avoids which buf pool still has free entries, but buf pools get added to
that array, which then would have its own head and tail. Obviously you
would need to do another pointer lookup to get the actual payload.

Obviously has one more pointer than just a single buf ring.


Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the freelist concept, which allows for dynamic buffer pool addition and removal, is what Miklos was referring to, and confirmed that the uapi changes in this series are forward-compatible with multi-bufpools.
reviews.raw_body:
I think the max-size array works but if it's backed by a linked list
instead of a fixed array, bufpool add/remove can be more dynamic (eg
no fixed ceiling). I think this is the freelist concept Miklos was
talking about. I don't think the implementation details really matter
right now though - all the kernel-internal stuff could be changed and
figured out after this series.

The uapi changes this series adds are forward-compatible with
multi-bufpools. It adds a uint16_t buf_id to fuse_uring_ent_in_out,
and there's already a uint16_t padding field right after it that a
pool_id can occupy later.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed concerns about documentation and commit messages by agreeing to fix them in v4.
reviews.raw_body:
I'll send out a v4 that adds Baokun's Reviewed-bys, fixes the
documentation typo, and adds a note to the zero-copy commit message
about abort teardown.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-06-11.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy to the FUSE file system, allowing for more efficient handling of I/O operations by reusing buffers instead of constantly allocating new ones.
